def: adj. Destitute of consideration or reflection, inconsiderate, unreflecting, thoughtless; unjust
more: m. adj. Destitute of consideration or reflection; unjust (woman or man).
subs: (f. -inī) an unjust man; an ill-advised person.

def: adv. (dakh.) See ابہي ab-hī.
n. m. Want of discrimination, thought, or consideration; thoughtlessness.
adj. Unreflecting; thoughtless; inconsiderate.
f. adj. Destitute of consideration or reflection; unjust (woman or man).
s. Water of the eyes; tears.
adj. = اوچهين avićhīn, q.v. adj. Without interval, close, dense, coarse, uninterrupted.

ety: act. part. of عبد 'to serve, worship,' &c.
def: s.m. A server, worshipper, adorer (of God), a votary, a devotee; a recluse.
more: n. m. عبد served. A worshipper; votary; devotee.
(part. act. of عبد Heb. To serve, to worship) s. m. An adorer, worshipper, votary, devotee.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عابد ('ābid), itself from Arabic عَابِد (ʕābid).

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻabd), Servants; worshippers.
feminine of عابِد (ʻābid, “believer; worshipper”)
s. Water of the mouth; spittle.

s. Sharpness and polish of the teeth. āb-dandān, 1. s. Watering of the mouth; weakness, trouble, crime or fault, a kind of pear, a kind of pomegranate, a kind of sweetmeat. 2. adj. Bad, weak, subjected, silly, base, in vain, useless.
adj. & adv. Void of, or contrary to, rule; irregular: irregularly.
s.f. = اوديا avidyā, q.v. s.f. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: adj. Unlearned, illiterate, ignorant; — s.m. An illiterate person.

def: s. Warm water.
s. Gravy, broth, water in which meat has been boiled down. (It is used in dressing pulāos ).
s. Water of life; name of the fabulous fountain of immortality.
s. Water of life; name of the fabulous fountain of immortality.
n. m. 1. Unclean or forbidden water; wine. 2. Hypocritical tears, crocodile's tears ( ṭaswe ).
more: s. Wine: the tears of a person who weeps hypocritically and sorrowless.

def: s. Water of desire; wish, appetite, longing.

n. m. 1. The water of life; the fountain of life; immortality. A fabulous spring hidden from human eyes. 2. The drinking-water used by a king. Jahāṅ panāh ke wāste ab-i-hayāt lāo . Ped. 3. Clear, cold, sweet water. Pānī kyā hai, āb-i-hayāt hai . Ped. 4. Inspiration; inspired knowledge. Rare .
more: s. Water of life; the fountain of life.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آب حیات (āb-i hayāt, literally “water of life”).

def: s.m. Bad grain; grain that does not germinate.
E. n. m. Seed that will not germinate; bad seed.
n. m. 1. Running water; a stream; rivulet. 2. Met . Tears running down the cheeks.
more: s. Running water; a stream, a rivulet, a current, tears flowing.

def: n. m. Ped. The waters of the river Kausar , fabled to flow in Paradise with milk and honey, or nectar.
more: s. The water of the river Kaus̤ar, fabled to flow in paradise with milk or nectar.

def: s. m. See ابشیک abishek.
s. The water of life: inspired knowledge.
adj. (f. -ā), Unapparent, imperceptible; hidden; undetermined, unknown; unintelligible. — abikt-ganit, s.f. The arithmetic of unknown quantities, algebra.
more: adj. Hidden, unintelligible; any invisible principle. ابکت گنت abikt ganit, s. f. Arithmetick of unknown quantities, algebra.

def: s.m. A blister, a pustule (of small pox, &c.). — ābla-i-farang, s.m. The European pox, syphilis; bubo.
more: n. m. 1. A blister; the pustule of small-pox.
s. m. A blister. آبله پا ābila-pā, adj. Having blisters on the feet. آبله پاٴي ābila-pā-ī, s. f. The having blisters on the feet. آبله فرنگ ābila-i-farang, s. m. The French pox, a bubo.

def: = اولمب avilamb, q.v. adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
s.m. Exemption from destruction, loss, or decay; safety.
more: s. m. Exemption from loss, safety.

def: n. m. S. नशृ to perish. Exemption from loss, or destruction; safety.
adj. (f. -inī), Indestructible, imperishable, exempt from decay or loss; everlasting, immortal; safe; entire
more: adj. Imperishable, exempt from decay, everlasting, eternal.

def: adj. A. lā-zavāl . 1. Imperishable; exempt from decay; everlasting; eternal; immortal. 2. Safe; entire; free from loss.
s.m. Want of good manners; indecorum; want of modesty; rude behaviour, rudeness, pertness; wantonness.
adj. Without point or dot.
adj. (f. -ā), Badly trained; ill-mannered; misbehaving, acting ill or improperly; unchaste, unfaithful (conjugally); wanton; pert, petulant: — s.m. An unfaithful spouse.
s.f. An immodest, unchaste, or unfaithful woman or wife.
n. m. Ped. Quicksilver; silver reduced by any menstruum to a liquid state ( chāndī kā pānī ).
more: Quicksilver, silver reduced by any menstruum to a liquid state.

def: s.m. Red powder used at the holī festival.
more: s. m. Powder used in the holī. See عبیر aﻌbīr.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Crossing, passing; — a passenger.
more: part. act, (of عبر Heb. ) Passing; (substant.) A passenger.

def: s.m. Saffron; ambergris; a mixture of perfumes compounded with saffron; a kind of powder used at the saturnalia of the holī (i.q. abīr).
more: s. m. Saffron, ambergris, powder of various colours used in the هولي holī.
s. m. A perfumed powder (of various colours used in the holī or on other occasions).

def: n. m. Ped. for H. bahtā pānī, lit . Running water. See āb-i-jārī . A kind of very fine muslin. Ābrawāṅ kā ḍoryā .
more: s. Running water; a fountain, the sky, a sort of extremely fine muslin.

def: adj. Of saffron, &c.; resembling saffron, &c.
more: adj. Of or like عبير aﻌbīr.

def: s.m. = اورودهہ avirodh, q.v.
s. m. Without contention; tranquillity, quietness.
adj. = اورودهي avirodhī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Not contentious or combative, peaceable, tranquil, quiet: — a peaceable person.
f. adj. Quiet, tranquil.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without desire, indifferent; without application
Cold water.

= ابهشيك abhishek, q.v. s.m. Sprinkling; inaugurating or consecrating by sprinkling water (usually that of the Ganges or other sacred river); inauguration of a king, royal unction; installation; religious bathing, ablution; bathing of the idol to which worship is offered; — a religious ceremony consisting of the presentation of a variety of articles (such as fruits, gems, &c.) along with water or with fluid substances for bathing the idol to which worship is offered.
more: from अभि and r. सिच Sprinkle) s. m. Bathing, baptizing, sprinkling with the water of the Ganges, initiation, royal unction; purification by means of sprinkling water and pronouncing certain prayers.

def: n. m. Sea water; salt water.
more: s. Brackish water, salt water, seawater.

def: = آبسته ābista, q.v. adj. Pregnant (a woman); with young (an animal); new-born (child).
adj. Pregnant (a woman); with young (an animal); new-born (child).
more: adj. Pregnant, with young (a woman or animal): new-born (a child). (Sans. आविष्ट Occupied, engrossed).

def: s.f. Pregnancy; the state of being with young.
adj. Pregnant, with young.
adj. Pregnant, with young.
s.f. Pregnancy; the state of being with young.
s.m. Want of confidence, distrust, disbelief; mistrust, suspicion.
more: s. m. Want of confidence, distrust, incredulity.

f. adj. Without confidence, distrustful.
f. adj. Without confidence, distrustful.
s.m. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unmarried, single

def: Water in which meat has been boiled down, gravy.
An infusion.
s.m. The nymphæa or lotus; the moon; a conch; the tree Barringtonia acutangula; Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods; a million millions.
more: s. m. The nymphæa or lotus: the moon: a conch: a kind of tree (eugenia acutangula): the physician of the gods.

def: s.m. 1˚. The first of a series of eight words comprising the letters of the Arabic alphabet in the order in which they were originally disposed (agreeing with that of the Hebrew and Aramaic). 2˚. The arrangement of the Arabic alphabet according to the occurrence of the letters in the said eight words. 3˚. The numerical value of the letters in this arrangement of them. 4˚. The alphabet. — abjad-ḵẖẉān, s.m. One who is learning the alphabet; a beginner (syn. nau-āmoz).
more: s. m. A particular arrangement of the Arabic alphabet; the alphabet; a mode of denoting numbers by the letters of the alphabet.

def: n. m. One who is learning his alphabet; a beginner.
s. m. One who is learning his alphabet.
s. f. A stream, a rivulet.
s. f. See آب جو āb-jō. ab-i-joʼe, The water of a rivulet.
n. m. 1. Gravy; soup ( shurvā ); broth; the juice of boiled meat. 2. n. f. A kind of raisin, or currant.
s.m. Rendering permanent; confirmation; establishment; perpetuation; prolongation; preservation.
more: (inf. iv. of بقي) s. m. Rendering permanent, confirmation, establishment, preservation.

def: n. m. P. آب water, کار work. A distiller or seller of spirituous liquors.
more: s. m. A water-carrier, a sprinkler, a distiller (especially of spirituous liquors), a wine-merchant or seller of spirituous liquors, a drinker of wine.

def: n. f. 1. The business of a distiller. 2. A distillery; a tavern; liquor shop. 3. A duty on the manufacture and sale of spirituous liquors; excise.
more: s. f. The business of a water-carrier, &c., the business of a distiller, distillation, a duty levied on distilleries.

def: s. m. A drawer of water.
adj. (f. -inī) & s.m. Without fruit; unfruitful, barren: — a tree without fruit.
adj. (comp. and super. of بخيل baḵẖīl), More, most or very covetous or avaricious.
more: adj. compar. (of بخیل) More, most or very covetous.

def: s. A repository of water.
s. f. A water-melon, musk-melon, or other fruit the inside of which is acid and perished.
s. 1. A soil in which water rises up if dug into but little. 2. A swell of water.
s.m. Vapours, exhalations, steam, fumes; — raving, delirium.
more: s. plur. (of بخار or بخور) Vapours, exhalations, perfumes.

def: n. m. P. آب water خر eat, drink, H. maṭkainā . An earthen or metal vessel for holding water; a goblet. Ek abkhorā ham ko bhī . Give me too a glass of water. ābkho're bhar'nā , Mah. To offer a libation of ābkhorās of sherbet or milk.
Drinking and eating; victuals, fortune, destiny.
more: s. m. Drinking, place of drinking water.

def: s. A narrow-mouthed vessel for drinking out of, a cup for drinking water.
Drinking and eating; victuals, fortune, destiny.
more: s. m. Drinking, place of drinking water.

def: adj. f. Of the present time, now.

a beautiful fairy, n. f. A young, weak, delicate, and beautiful woman; a belle; an unprotected female. 1. Sāukār kī ablā beṭī, baṛī woh chātar re . Song. Lāonī charīttar kī . 2. Kahā na ablā kar sake, kahā na sindh samāë? Kahā na pāwak meṅ jare, kahā kāl nā khāë? Riddle (Bujhavval). Sut na ablā kar sake, aur man na sindh samāë, Dharam na pāwak meṅ jare, nām kāl nā khāë . Ans. Būjh . What is't a belle can't make, what is't no ocean can contain? What is't no fire can burn, what death itself can't swallow up? No belle (alone) can make a son, no ocean can contain the mind; Integrity no fire can burn, nor death devour fair name and fame.
communique
to impart
adj. Simple, silly, foolish; ignorant; bashful. — ablah-fareb, s.m. A sly, wily knave; an impostor.
more: (from بله Heb. בלה Shook; hence, wavered from mental imbecility, was old or worn out) adj. Foolish, silly, ignorant, bashful.

def: s.f. Silliness, foolishness, folly.
n. m. The European pock; bubo; syphilis (ātshak) .
adj. Black and white, party-coloured, piebald; spotted; pepper-and-salt; grey.
more: (from بلق) adj. Piebald, black and white, party-coloured.
wety: From Arabic أَبْلَق (ʔablaq), itself from Persian ابلک.

def: adj. Piebald; black and white; red and white; party-coloured; spotted; gray. Prov. Wohī kannyā, jis ke ablaq bāl! A virgin, and gray hairs! (Expressive of something very wonderful or impossible).
s.m. A bird of the maina kind; a starling.
more: s. m. A bird of the mainā kind, a starling.

def: s.m. White sugar-candy.
more: s. White sugarcandy, or powdered loaf sugar.

ety: pl. of ابن ibn; — used in comp. only
def: s.m. Sons; people; tribes. — abnā-i-jins, s.m. Those of the same rank, quality, kind, or species; companions, comrades, equals. abnā-i-dahr, s.m. and abnā-i-rozgār, s.m. Sons of the time, contemporaries.
strait
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبنای (ābnāy, “creek”), compound of آب (āb, “water”) + نای (nāy, “lane, throat, pipe”).

def: n. f. The perpendicular tube of a huqqā on which the chilam is fixed.
( Geog .) A strait.
s. The perpendicular tube of a ḥuḳḳa on which the chilam is fixed.
s. plur. Sons of the age or time, contemporaries.
s. plur. Of the same quality or rank; comrades, companions, equals.
s. plur. Sons of the age or time, contemporaries.
s.f. The earth, world.
more: s. m. Pertness, wantonness, want of submission.

def: s.m. Ebony. — ābnūs-kā kundā, adj. (Lit. 'a log of ebony'); black and ill-featured, repulsive: — s.m. A repulsive man; a blackamoor.
more: n. m. Ebony; Diospyros ebenum , Wat. āb'nūs kā kun'dā , adj. lit . a log of ebony. Black; ill-featured; repulsive. Ādmī kyā hai, ābnūs kā kundā haī . —n. m. Met . A blackamoor.

def: adj. Made of obony; black as ebony; black.
s.m. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: (f. -ā), Unintelligent, stupid, ignorant; puzzled, perplexed. — abodh-gamya, adj. (f. -ā), Incomprehensible.

def: adj. (f. -ā), not to be understood, unintelligible, incomprehensible.
adj. = ابدهہ abudh and ابودهہ abodh, q.v. adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.
atmosphere, climate; water and air
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آب و هوا (āb-u-hawā, literally “water and air”).

ety: अ + rt. of बोलना bolnā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ā), Speechless; silent, mute; dumb.
adj. (f. -ī), Disposed or accustomed to be silent, &c. ( = abol); taciturn; reserved; conceited, vain
more: n. m. A child who cannot speak distinctly.
adj. m. Disposed or accustomed to silence.
subs: s.m. Dumb creature; one incapable of speaking distinctly

def: adj. S वू to speak. 1. Disposed, or accustomed to silence; silent. 2. Speechless; mute; dumb; mum. 3. Vain; conceited; overweening.

n. m. lit . Water and color. 1. Beauty; polish; gloss; sheen; splendour. 2. The highest degree of pleasure; bliss. Jis ne is dunyā meṅ ā-kar ek din bhī pī na bhaṅg. Us ne, sach pūchho to, kyā dekhā jahāṅ kā āb o raṅg? Nazīr. He who hath come into this world, and not once tasted bhaṅg , To tell the truth, what hath he known of this world's sparkling joys?
s. The stars.
n. f. Irrigation; watering fields (H. pānī paṭānā ).
cloudy, nubilous, unclear, uncleared
nubecula
v. n. To be overcast; to lower; to be cloudy; to threaten rain ( bādal chhānā ).
nebulous

ety: Old P. awar, Pehl. abar, Zend awra, S. अभ्र
def: s.m. A cloud; the marks on the paper called abrī (q.v.). — abr-i-āẕur, s.m. A cloud without rain. — abr ćhānā, v.n. Clouds to overshadow; to be cloudy or overcast; to lower. — abr-i-ʻālamgīr, s.m. Rain clouds which cover a large extent of country. — abr-i-qibla, s.m. Clouds which come from the direction of the qibla; clouds heavily charged with rain. — abr khulnā, v.n. Clouds to disperse; (the sky) to be clear. — abr-i-murda, s.m. A sponge.
more: n. m. S. अभ्र. A cloud (bādal). abr khul'nā , v. n. To disperse (clouds); to be clear (the sky). Abr khulā, havā chalī, būṅdeṅ thamīṅ, sahar huī; Pahlū se yār uth gayā, sab woh bahār bah gaī . Nazīr. The clouds dispersed, the wind arose, the rain had ceased, the morning broke, My mistress from my arms had gone, the sweet spring time had glided by.
s. m. A cloud. ابرسیاه abr-i-siyāh, A black cloud. ابرغلیظ abr-i-g̠ẖalīz̤, A thick cloud.

def: s.m. The outer fold of a double garment (opposed to استر astar, 'the lining'). — abrā ćaṛhānā (-par). To sow or quilt another, or outer, fold (upon a garment).
more: s. m. The outer fold of a double garment (Sans. अभ्र abhra ) . See ابره abra.

def: s.m. = ابرا abrā, q.v. s.m. The outer fold of a double garment (opposed to استر astar, 'the lining'). — abrā ćaṛhānā (-par). To sow or quilt another, or outer, fold (upon a garment).
more: s. m. The outer cloth of a double garment. See ابرا abrā.

def: v. a. To sew on or quilt another, or outer fold.
to indemnify
n. m. P. بر and اُبر upon, above. The outer fold, in contradistinction to the astar or lining of the cloth.
adj. (comp. & super. of برد), More, most, or very cold.
more: adj. compar. (from برد Heb. and Ch. כךך To freeze, to hail) Cold, very cold.

def: = the next Arabicized.
more: (from ابرک Heb. and Ch. כךק To lighten, to shine or sparkle. Sans. अभ्रक abhrak ) s. m. Talk, mica.

def: s.m. Talc; mica.
n. S. अभ्रक. Talc; mica (bhoḍal) .
s.m. = آبهرن ābharan, q.v. s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)

n. m. S. आभरण ornament, आ all round, भृ to wear. Jewels (gahnā); ornaments; fine dress; finery. 1. Abran naë naë . New dresses. 2. Sonā sunār kā, abhran sansār kā . Prov. The ornament is the wearer's, but the gold remains with the goldsmith. It is said that a king once asked a goldsmith, how many annas in the rupee he was wont to appropriate. He replied, "the whole sixteen annas." To put him to the test, the king ordered him to make a golden image, and caused a strict watch to be placed over him night and day during the execution of the work. Before entering on his task, within the palace walls, the goldsmith made a brass image which he left at his own house. When the golden image was made, the goldsmith said to the watchmen, "Now one thing only is wanting. It must be immersed in an acid." Accordingly, by previous appointment, as his wife passed the palace gate, crying " le dahī " (who will buy my tyre), she was called in, and the goldsmith managed to put into the pot of tyre the golden image for the brass one which he took out of it.
s.m. Bracelet; ankle ornament of gold or silver (cf. abran).
s.m. Bracelet; ankle ornament of gold or silver (cf. abran).
s.m. Just, holy, pious men; saints: dutiful (to parents).
more: s. plur. (from برّ Heb. and Ch. כךך To polish, purify, select) Just, holy, pious men, dutiful (to parents).

def: adj. Leprous.
more: (from برص) adj. Leprous, having the leprosy.

def: adj. Marked with small specks, speckled: mottled; dapple grey (horse).

ety: for A. عبرة, inf. n. of un. fr. عبر 'to shed tears'
def: s.f. Weeping; grief.
n. m. Raw silk, the cocoon (koyā) of which is given with other medicines by hakīms .
s.m. Raw silk, silk; sewing silk.
more: s. m. Silk, sewing silk, raw silk.

def: adj. Silken, made of silk.
adj. Clouded; mottled; variegated
more: adj. Clouded, variegated. ابري کاغذ abr-ī kāg̠ẖaẕ, A kind of thick and shining paper, clouded (from Cashmir).
subs: (also abrī kāg̠aẕ), a kind of thick and shining paper, clouded (obtained from Kashmīr); variegated, or marble paper.

def: n. f. آبر a cloud, lit . Clouded; mottled. Variegated or marble paper.

ety: Pehl. brū, Z. brvat, S. भ्रु
def: s.f. Eye-brow. — Abrū-par girī mārnī, To knit the brow, to frown.
more: s. f. The eyebrow. ابرو پر گره مارنا abrū par girih mārnā, v. a. To frown.

def: (i.e. āb-i-rū), v.s.v. آب āb.
more: n. f. آب brightness, رو face. 1. Honour; character; reputation. 1. Ābrū jag meṅ rahe, to jān jānā pashm hai . Prov. Your honour keep, your life count as your pubes flung away. 2. Ābrū dunyā meṅ, yāro, motī kī sī āb hai . Tan-durustī, aur bhī phir aish kā asbāb hai . Nazīr. The brightness of the pearl is honour in this world, my friends, It's health, and all the stock and furniture of pleasure too. 2. Rank; dignity; name ( nām ). Ab unke pās kyā rahā, apnī ābrū lȳe baiṭhe haiṅ . He hasn't a farthing left; his rank is all he sits in. 3. Position; show; appearance; magnificence. Is gaē guzre waqt meṅ bhī apnī ābrū banā rakhī hai . Wom. Even in his declining fortune he supports his position. 4. Support; stay; credit; ornament; pride. Yehī hamāre gharāne kī ābrū hai . ābrū' utār'nā,—bigāṛ'nā,—le'nā , v. a. 1. To disgrace; abuse; call names; put to shame; to destroy one's good name; take away…

s. f. Honour, character, reputation, renown, elegance. Name of a poet. آبرو اتارني ābrū utārnī, v. a. To dishonour, to disgrace. آبروبرباد دیني ābrū bar-bād denī, v. a. To lose oneʼs own character, to destroy that of another. آبرودیني ābrū denī, v. a. To impart honour or reputation to any one, or to lose or surrender oneʼs own. آبروکرني ābrū karnī, v. a. To treat with respect. آبرو لیني ābrū lenī, v. a. To take away character or reputation.

def: s.m. A vineyard; a garden.
more: s. m. A vineyard, a garden.

def: s.m. (pl. of بصر), Views, looks; perception, understanding.
more: s. plur. (of بصر) Eyes, sights, understanding.

def: (an expression of those who, after reciting past misfortune, pray to be preserved from a return of it) Far be it from us!
s. m. f. A waterfall, cascade, cataract.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبشار (ābšār), in turn derived from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭡𐭱𐭠𐭥 (ābšār). By surface analysis, آب (āb, “water”) + شار (śār, “pour”), see Persian شاریدن (šārīdan) for the second part.

def: s. m. The sailor who heaves the lead in sounding.
s. (dakh.) See آب شوره āb-shora (Qanoon-e-islam).
n. m. 1. Water cooled with saltpetre. E. 2. A sherbet made of lime juice and sugar.
more: s. Lemonade, or a species of negus without the wine ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. f. 1. Splendour; lustre; brilliance; brightness; radiance; sheen. Unke chehre kī āb-tāb se āftāb bhī āb āb hai . Ped. 2. Magnificence; grandeur; sublimity; dignity; majesty; glory; pomp.
adv. Presently. اب تب هونا ab-tab-honā, To be at the point of death.
n. m. A jug; an ewer with a spout.
more: s. m. An ewer, a jug: see آفتابه āftāba.

def: adv. (dakh.) Till now.
adv. Till now.

adv. 1. Yet; as yet Dikhyo, jī, ablo to ham halwāt meṅ na baṭṭhe . Jāt. Look now, to this day I have never been kept in the havālāt . 2. Hitherto; till now; heretofore; up to this time. 3. All the time; all along; all this while.
more: adv. Till now, hitherto, yet, as yet, still.

def: adj. (comp. & super. of باطل bāt̤il), More, most, or very vain or fruitless, &c.
more: adj. compar. (of باطل) More, or most vain or fruitless.

def: adv. Till now, hitherto, yet, as yet, still.

W. v. a. 1. To rub, or knead the body with abṭan . This is a customary rite before marriage, and the intention is, not only to soften and cleanse the skin, but also, according to some, to close the pores so as to increase the natural heat of the body and stimulate it for the consummation of the marriage. This object is further promoted by the jokes, etc. of the female relatives who rub the bride and bridegroom all over with abṭan . The latter portion of the description is said to apply to certain classes of people only. 1. Dūlā mīyāṅ khudā ke wāste mere ubṭan na malnā . Wom. 2. Abaṭī chabaṭī dulhā kailoṅ syān . Wom. E. Mag. Song. With rubbing abṭan , and all that, the bridegroom has been brought up to the mark. 2. To anoint (a king).

ety: from بتر 'to cut off,' 'to cut off a tail'
def: adj. Ruined, spoiled; deteriorated, vitiated; worthless, destitute of good qualities, dissolute, wanton, disorderly; destitute, miserable, poor; in disorder, disarranged; defective, imperfect; false (as a deal at cards). — abtar karnā, v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, deprave, corrupt; to derange, disarrange, disorder, to shuffle (a pack of cards).
more: (from بتر Was destitute of a tail: Heb. בתר Cut short) adj. Ruined, spoiled, worthless, destitute of good qualities, dissolute, disorderly, disordered, wanton. ابترکرنا abtar karnā, To ruin, spoil, disorder.

def: v. n. See kharāb' ho'nā . 1. To be or become deteriorated; to deteriorate; degenerate; to be the worse for; to retrograde. 2. To be demoralized; to be debauched. 3. To be in disorder, topsy-turvy, disarranged.
to disturb
v. a. 1. To spoil; damage; impair; mar. 2. To corrupt; deprave; vitiate ( bigāṛnā ). 3. To derange; jumble; upset. 4. To shuffle (the cards). Ganifā abtar karo .
s.f. Deterioration, ruin, decay; worthlessness, wretchedness, poverty; disorder, confusion; disorganization; mismanagement.
more: n. f. 1. Disorder; derangement; disorganization; irregularity; confusion; disarrangement. 2. Deterioration; decay; falling off. Din ba-din abtarī hotī jātī hai . 3. Mal-administration; mismanagement.

def: to disconcert
adv. From this time, henceforth.
adv. Till now, hitherto, yet, as yet, still.
adv. Till now, hitherto, yet, as yet, still.
(lit. father of the virgin) n. prop. The father-in-law and first successour to Muhammad.
(father of the cat, because he was in the habit of carrying a cat with him) n. prop. One of the companions of Muhammad.
(father of ignorance) n. prop. An uncle of Muhammad.
n. prop. Ali, the son-in-law of Muhammad.
Abu Dhabi (an emirate of the United Arab Emirates, on the southern Persian Gulf); Abu Dhabi (the capital city of the United Arab Emirates; the capital city of Abu Dhabi emirate, United Arab Emirates)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَبُو ظَبْي (ʔabū ẓaby). Spelling likely influenced from English Abu Dhabi.

ety: used, like ibn, in comb. with a following subst. in the gen., the final ū being shortened in pron. if the Ar. art. precede the subst.
def: s.m. Father; originator, author, &c. — abuʼl-bashar, Lit. 'The father of the human race'; Adam. — abuʼl-ḥāris̤, s.m. The lion (he being among beasts of prey, the strongest to acquire). — abū-bakr, Lit. 'The father of the virgin'; the name of the father-in-law of, and first successor to, Mohammad. — abū-turāb, Lit. 'Father dusty;' a nickname of 'Alī, the son-in-law of Mohammad. — abū-jahl, Lit. 'Father of ignorance,' the name of an uncle of Mohammad, noted for his stubbornness. — abū-ʻalī-sīnā, The name of a celebrated sage and physician. — abū-huraira, Lit. 'Father (owner) of the little cat'; the nickname of one of Mohammad's disciples (who was in the habit of carrying a pet cat about with him).
adj. = abudh, q.v.
subs: s.f. Want of understanding or sense

def: adj. (f. -mati), = abudh and abuddhi (adj.) qq.v. adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.
adj. Deficient in, or void of, understanding or sense, unwise, ignorant, stupid, foolish: — s.m. A fool.

W. adj. S. बुध् to know. 1. Ignorant; stupid; uninformed. Nipaṭ abudh, samjhai kahā budh jan bachan bilās? Wom. E. An arrant fool! He has no idea of the pleasurable amatory talk of puberty. [Usually said by the prurient bride to the boy bridegroom not yet grown to puberty.] 2. Young; thoughtless; knowing nothing; innocent. Bābā ghar rahloṅ bahute abodhwā . Ab to maiṅ bhailoṅ sīanawā . Mag. Wom. Song. My childhood's home saw me a simple innocent. And now behold I'm grown a knowing woman quite. 3. Inexperienced; raw; not ripe. 4. Not yet arrived at the age of puberty. Morā laṛikauā abodhwā . Mag. Wom. My child is not marriageable yet.
more: E. adj. S. अ not, बिध् to rule. Irregular; informal; deranged. —adv. Irregularly; informally.

def: adj. S. बुध् to know, H. būjh understand. Void of, or deficient in understanding or sense. Andher nagrī, abūjh rājā. Ṭake ser kakṛī, take ser khājā! Prov. E. Injustice in the city, a king without understanding. For a cucumber you shall pay the price of a khājā .
adj. (f. -ā), Void of, or deficient in, understanding or sense; senseless, stupid.
adj. (f. -ī), Not understood; incomprehensible.
(father of excellence) n. prop. The secretary of the Emperor Akbar.

s.m. Doors, gates; chapters, sections; heads or subjects of taxation; sources of public revenue, such as cesses, imposts, assessments on land, &c.: (under Mohammadan rule) taxes imposed in excess of the original assessment.
n. m. pl. of Z. babā , باب door, chapter. 1. Chapters; sections. (Rare). 2. Taxes imposed by the Mahomedan government in excess of the original assessment; cesses; heads or subjects of taxation.
(from ابو) adj. Paternal.
s.m. = اويو avyava, q.v.
s. f. A species of pigeon: a sort of very fine garment or cloth.
s. f. Irrigation, watering.
s.f. pl. (of بيت bait), Lit. 'houses'; distichs, verses.
more: s. plur. (of بیت) Houses; distichs or verses (most commonly).

more: adj. compar. (of بیض r. باض Heb. and Ch. Shone or was exceeding white) White, whiter, more or most splendid or shining.

n. m. In comp. A mountain, as Himāchal, Bindyāchal .
adj. Immovable, incapable of motion; s. m. A mountain, an aged person. نیل اچل nīl achal, The blue mountain near Balasore.

s.m. Rinsing the mouth; the act of sipping a little water from the palm of the hand by way of purification before meals or religious ceremonies
more: n. m. S. आचम् to rinse, sip. 1. Washing the hands and mouth; rinsing. Bhojan kare, puni achwan kīnhā . Brij. 2. The act of sipping a little water from the palm of the hand by way of purification before meals, or religious ceremonies. āch'man kar'nā , W. achā'nā , E. v. n. See āchman .
s. m. Act of sipping a little water from the palm of the hand by way of purification.

def: = اچمبا aćambā, q.v. s.m. Wonderful thing, wonder, marvel; wonder, surprise, astonishment, amazement, bewilderment, perplexity
subs: (f. -ī), Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, surprising; astounding; extraordinary, uncommon, strange, rare
(-se), To marvel (at), be astonished, surprised, astounded, &c. (at)
(or aćambe-)kā, adj. (f. -ī), Surprising, strange, extraordinary, uncommon, new, &c.
(or aćambe-) meṅ ānā, and aćambe honā, To be lost in wonder, astonishment, or amazement; to be astounded, bewildered, perplexed, &c.

def: s.m. Wonderful thing, wonder, marvel; wonder, surprise, astonishment, amazement, bewilderment, perplexity
more: n. m. S. अ not, चम्ब् to go. 1. Wonder; astonishment; surprise; consternation. 1. Āwat, jāt (sāṅs) na dīkhe, ye bhī ek achambā! Kabīr. (The breath) going and coming none can see, this too is strange. 2. Dekho achambe kī bāt, hījṛe ke beṭā bhayo! Rus. Prov. A wonder hear, a eunuch's got a son! 2. Strange, or new matter (achambe kī bāt); a strange feeling. Āh o zārī jo koī kartā thā, Ek achambā hameṅ guzartā thā . Shauq. If any one did weep and wail, It seemed to me so wondrous strange! acham'bā , adj. 1. Extraordinary; rare; uncommon; curious; strange. 2. Astonishing; surprising; amazing; astounding; startling; prodigious; wonderful; wondrous; marvellous. acham'be ho'nā,—meṅ ā'nā , v. n. To be lost in wonder or astonishment; be puzzled; not to know what to do; to be at a loss. Phr. To look blank; to take away one's breath; to stagger belief, or stand aghast; to turn up the eyes. Jab Rāwan…
subs: (f. -ī), Wonderful, marvellous, astonishing, surprising; astounding; extraordinary, uncommon, strange, rare
(-se), To marvel (at), be astonished, surprised, astounded, &c. (at)
(or aćambe-)kā, adj. (f. -ī), Surprising, strange, extraordinary, uncommon, new, &c.
(or aćambe-) meṅ ānā, and aćambe honā, To be lost in wonder, astonishment, or amazement; to be astounded, bewildered, perplexed, &c.

more: n. m. S. चर् to live, Pālī. āchāro conduct. 1. Manner of life; conduct; behaviour ( chāl chalan ). 2. Religious observance; essential rites or ceremonies, as daily ablution, and personal purification ( achār bichār ). 3. Usage; custom ( rīt ); practice.

n. m. Pickles. achār banā'nā—ḍāl'nā , v. a. 1. To pickle; make pickles. 2. Ironically . To preserve in pickle. Tum kyā achār ḍāloge? (Applied to superfluities, or an excess of any thing.) achār kar' nā,—nikāl'nā , v. a. To beat severely; to beat to a jelly. Phr. To make mince meat of. 1. Mujh se tīn pāṅch lāë, to achār nikāl dūṅgā . 2. Mārte mārte achār kar dȳā . achārī, achār kī hāṅḍī , n. f. A jar for containing pickles.
s. m. Established rule of conduct, custom, practice, institute, religious observance.
subs: adj. Irregular, out of rule; out of caste; — s.m. An outcast.

def: s.m. = آچاريه āćārya, q.v. s.f. The wife of an āćārya; a spiritual preceptress.
more: n. m. S. आचार्य, , Pālī āchariyo . 1. A Spiritual guide or teacher, especially one who invests the student with the sacrificial thread, and instructs him in the Vedas, and in the law of sacrifice and religious mysteries. 2. A great pandit ; a master of science; the title affixed to names of learned men. 3. A sect of Brahmans who perform the obsequies of the dead ( burā bāman ).
s. m. A spiritual guide or teacher, a learned panḍit.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Following established rites and practices, observant of religious ceremonies; religious, pious; — one who follows established rules and practices, &c.; a religious man; a devotee.
more: n. m. 1. One who is strict in the observance of religious ceremonies. 2. A devotee; a pious, holy man; a fanatic. āchā'rī , adj. Practising what is good, pious, holy, devout.
adj. Practising (what is good), strict in the observance of religious ceremonies.

Guide or instructor in religious matters, especially one who invests the student with the sacrificial thread and instructs him in the Vedas, in the law of sacrifice and the mysteries of religion; priest; founder, or leader of a sect; a learned paṇḍit; a title affixed to the names of learned men.
more: s. m. A spiritual preceptor or a teacher in matters of religion.

adj. Destitute of consciousness; unconscious; inanimate; insensible; senseless; fainting; out of one's mind; thoughtless; heedless, careless, regardless; inattentive; off one's guard; negligent; forgetful; stupid
more: adj. S. चित् to attend to. 1. Inattentive; absent; abstracted. 1. Chet re, a-chet yār, Samajh būjh raṭ Murār . Bhajan. 2. Achet nāhit kāhe kare lā? Dekhā na nīke . Mag. Why do you look about? Fix your attention properly. 2. Regardless; careless; heedless; reckless ( be-parvā ). Aisan achet ghoṛā haklan, ki laṛkā pichā gel . Bhoj. He drove so furionsly that he ran over the child. 3. Neglectful; negligent; forgetful; remiss ( bhulakkaṛ ). Baṛā a chet hai, jis kām ko kahte haiṅ bhūl jātā hai . 4. Not circumspect; off one's guard; sound asleep; napping; incautious ( bekhabar ). Aise achet ho ke soë, chorī ho gaī . 5. Inconsiderate; thoughtless ( be-fikr ). Ye buṛbak achet, je jī meṅ āël se bak lak . Mag. This wandering fool babbles away just as it comes into his head. 6. Insensible (as in a swoon); senseless ( be-hosh ).
adj. Out of mind or senses, senseless, thoughtless, stupified.
subs: v.n. To be thoughtless, be insensible (to); to become or be insensible, or in a swoon, &c.

def: adj. = اچيت aćet; and = اچيتنيه aćaitanya, qq.v. adj. Destitute of consciousness; unconscious; inanimate; insensible; senseless; fainting; out of one's mind; thoughtless; heedless, careless, regardless; inattentive; off one's guard; negligent; forgetful; stupid

def: s. m. Cloth, garments.
n. m. S. अक्षर, अ not, क्षर to perish, Pālī. akkharam . A letter of the alphabet; letter; character. beh mātā ke achchhar, karam rekh , n. Predestination; fate; destiny; the hand of destiny.

n. m. S. अ not, क्षत broken, Pālī. akkhatam . 1. Whole or unbroken rice used in oblations. 2. A pinchful of rice (just enough for pūjā ). Hāṅḍī meṅ achchhat nā, chalā samdhī jeṅve . Mag. Prov. Not a pinch of rice in the pot, and he says "come and feast with me." achchhat tilak , The ceremony of putting a few grains of rice on the forehead of an image or of a Brāhman when invited to an entertainment.

n. m. an Emp. form of ach'chhā . 1. Betters; superiors. 1. Maiṅ to tumhāre achchhoṅ se le lūṅgā . 2. Achchhe achchhoṅ ko dekh lȳā hai, terī kyā asal hai? I have seen your betters, who are you? 2. Patrons; supporters; helpers ( murabbī ). 3. Fathers; forefathers; heads; ancestors; predecessors; progenitors ( baṛe būṛhe ). 4. Men of birth, rank, or authority; noblemen; gentlemen; lords; great men; rich men; gentle folk; magnates; grandees ( baṛe ādmī ). Achchhe achchhe māre phirte haiṅ . Good men are knocking about (for want of bread). 5. Men of ability, mental or physical power, learned men; literati . Achchhe achchhoṅ ke uṛā detī hai shamshīr havās . Zafar. 6. Ironic. Bad men; men of low origin. Kisī achchhe kā ṭol hai !
s.m. Throwing away; convulsion; a putting upon; reproof, censure, blame; sarcasm, irony.
adj. (dakh.) Good, &c.: see اچها achchhā.
adj. (dakh.) Being, existing.
adj. plur. Good. See اچها achchhā.

n. f. Causal form from S. अक्षर. 1. Orthography. 2. A mode of playing on a stringed instrument so as to express the words of a song ( bol bajāna ). 3. A Turkish guitar, lute, or mandolin, with a long neck and usually having three to seven strings.
v. n. See اچهتاناپچهتانا achhtānā pachhtānā.

adj. S. अ not, छुप् to touch. 1. Untouched; unpolluted; untasted. Rasoī achhūtī hai . 2. Not used; quite new. Bichhāūṅ soznī † ek ujlī aur achhūtīlā . Wom. Rangin. 3. Fresh; new; not broached. Tujhe gāfil nashe meṅ jab pāyā, Phir achhūtā kisī ne kab chhoṛā? Nazīr. Hath any found you drunk, And yet left you untouched? 4. Holy; not to be touched (offerings). Is ko hāth na lagāo, achhūtā hai. achhūtā bartan , n. lit . An untouched vessel. A maid; virgin; unbroached girl. M/?/āṅ, yeh achhūtā bartan hai, rāt bhar ke do saw leṅge .
old-shoe
s.m. Ulcers in the throat, the thrush. (Cf. چهارو ćhārū.)
n. To be, exist; to remain, abide, stay.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To be, to exist, to remain: see اچهنا achhnā.

def: n. To be, exist; to remain, abide, stay.
more: v. n. To be, exist, become, continue.

def: n. f. Restlessness; playfulness; vivacity.
s.m. Calumnious accusation; calumny.
more: A false accusation; a calumny. Are, jhūṭoṅ matī jorū achhraṅgyā re kī . Mag. Horil . Song. Now, don't make up false charges.

def: s.f. The music of a stringed instrument played so as to express the words of a song. (See next.)
s.f. Orthography; — a inode of playing upon a stringed instrument so as to express the words of a song; — a Turkish guitar, lute or mandoline.
more: s. f. Orthography; a mode of playing on a stringed instrument so as to express the words of a song.

def: v.n. To repent, regret; to grieve, repine.
more: v. n. lit . To advance and retire. To wish and not to wish; pine and repine. Astā pastā-kar wohī roṭī khāī .
(see پچهتانا) v. n. To repent, to grieve, to be sorry.

def: an untouchable; outlaw, pariah, outcast
part. adj. (f. -ī), Not to be touched, intangible (as food for religious persons, &c.); inviolable; — untouched, untasted; unpolluted, undefiled; unblemished; clean, pure; unused, new.
more: (see اجوٿها) adj. Intangible (victuals dressed for saints, &c.), inviolable.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀙𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (achutta), from Sanskrit अच्छुप्त (acchupta).

def: direct/oblique/vocative feminine singular/plural of اچھوتا (achūtā)
s.f. A caudle or gruel given to puerperal women. (See ajwāʼinī).
more: n. f. From ajwān a kind of aniseed. An aromatic decoction given to women after child-birth.

def: adj. Unpolished, rough.
adj. Undistinguished; without note or consequence.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Thoughtless, regardless, heedless, careless, rash; — a thoughtless person, &c.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, heedlessness, inconsiderateness, disregard, carelessness, unconcern; negligence, neglect.
s.f. Thoughtlessness, heedlessness, inconsiderateness, disregard, carelessness, unconcern; negligence, neglect.
more: W. n. f. S. चिन्त् to think. 1. Thoughtlessness; inattention; inconsiderateness ( be-fikrī ). 2. Neglect; negligence; disregard ( bhūl ). 3. Heedlessness; carelessness; remissness ( be-parvāī ).

def: part. adj. Not thought of; unexpected; disregarded.
adj. Not of long duration, brief; transitory, evanescent; not of long date, recent.
adj. Unwished for, undesired.
more: adj. 1. ( अ and r. चित Think) Unwished for. 2. ( अ and r. चित्र Paint) Not painted (paper, &c.).
